purpose: Use biomedical ontology crosswalks as additional weak evidence for health-policy concept alignment during Track 13 silver labelling.
scope_guard: Ontology matches may support topic/concept triangulation, but they do not by themselves prove claim, premise, stance, support, or attack labels.

primary_hpo_sources.name: Human Phenotype Ontology downloads
primary_hpo_sources.use: HPO OBO/OWL files include UMLS-created cross-references such as UMLS and SNOMEDCT_US xrefs.

primary_hpo_sources.name: NLM UMLS HPO source representation
primary_hpo_sources.use: Documents HPO processing in the UMLS Metathesaurus.

crosswalk_ontologies.name: Unified Medical Language System
crosswalk_ontologies.mapping_relation: HPO is represented in UMLS; HPO files include UMLS xrefs.

crosswalk_ontologies.name: SNOMED CT
crosswalk_ontologies.mapping_relation: SNOMED International and Monarch collaborate on HPO to SNOMED CT and SNOMED CT to HPO maps.

crosswalk_ontologies.name: Medical Subject Headings
crosswalk_ontologies.mapping_relation: Can be derived through UMLS mappings for some HPO phenotype terms.

crosswalk_ontologies.name: MedDRA
crosswalk_ontologies.mapping_relation: Appears in HPO/UMLS cross-reference workflows for phenotype terminology.

crosswalk_ontologies.name: ICD diagnosis vocabularies
crosswalk_ontologies.mapping_relation: Useful where UMLS links clinical diagnosis codes to HPO terms, but coverage is incomplete.

silver_vote_policy.gold_label_warning: Ontology matches are weak silver evidence only and must not be represented as human labels.
